tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and microalbuminuria, indicating significant renal dysfunction. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting possible tubular injury or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂. LDL cholesterol is elevated, increasing cardiovascular risk. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes of renal impairment.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
